stakeholders.Role: Provides guidance on guardianship orders

stakeholders.Role: Handles court appointed committee for mentally incapacitated persons

Source metadata.extraction_context: Committee of Estate of a Mentally Incapacitated Person appointed by the Court, you can visit the Hong Kong Judiciary website: www.judiciary.hk/en/court_services_facilities/guidance_note.html

stakeholders.Role: Supports people living with dementia

Source metadata.extraction_context: We are proud to be working in partnership with Hong Kong Alzheimer’s Disease Association. Together we are building a more dementia- friendly community...

stakeholders.Role: Leading financial services group in Asia with a commitment to sustainability

Source metadata.extraction_context: DBS is a leading financial services group in Asia with a presence in 19 markets. Headquartered and listed in Singapore, DBS is in the three key Asian axes of growth: Greater China, Southeast Asia and South Asia.

stakeholders.Role: Supports social enterprises and community causes, as well as employee volunteerism

Source metadata.extraction_context: DBS Foundation awarded SGD 3.7 million in grant funding to 24 SMEs and social enterprises... to build, support, and sustain a robust community of senior women leaders and strengthen our pipeline for senior leadership in the bank.

stakeholders.Role: Connector between social enterprises and businesses to create impact

Source metadata.extraction_context: Regular events and conversations with Businesses for Impact, community partners and social service agencies to understand the social landscape and how DBS can partner to create impact.

stakeholders.Role: Upskilling SMEs and strengthening their sustainability skills

Source metadata.extraction_context: For example, we partnered with SkillsFuture Singapore, a Singapore government agency, to upskill SMEs and help strengthen their sustainability skills through the DBS SME Skills Booster programme.

stakeholders.Role: Regulates financial institutions and supports green financing initiatives

Source metadata.extraction_context: DBS actively participated in several MAS projects Transition and working groups, such as on managed coal phase out.

stakeholders.Role: Operates as a leading financial services group in Asia focusing on sustainable finance.

Source metadata.extraction_context: As part of the master plan, we have introduced a robust set of initiatives for leadership capability development. To drive leadership effectiveness, we have embedded the six transformational leadership tenets - growth mindset, psychological safety, feedback culture...

stakeholders.Role: Facilitates networking opportunities for professionals in finance.

Source metadata.extraction_context: Many also appreciate the opportunity to connect with colleagues from different departments and learn from the diversity of experiences. Acknowledging the value of building networks, we also facilitated networking opportunities for our Circle leaders and members to meet professionals from external organisations via our corporate membership in the Financial Women's Association of Singapore...

stakeholders.Role: Collaborates to support women's professional development.

Source metadata.extraction_context: We also facilitated networking opportunities for our Circle leaders and members to meet professionals from external organisations via our corporate membership in the Financial Women's Association of Singapore and partnering with the Temasek Women's Network...

stakeholders.Role: Involved in discussions about inclusive leadership and gender equity in workplaces.

Source metadata.extraction_context: To deepen awareness and understanding, we also organised bankwide events such as DBS International Women’s Day which featured a keynote speech by Minister Shanmugam of the Singapore Ministry of Home Affairs and Law highlighting the mindset shifts that must occur for further progress in women’s development...

stakeholders.Role: Engages in sustainability reporting and compliance with environmental, social, and governance (ESG) standards.

Source metadata.extraction_context: From Ambition to Action DBS Group Holdings Ltd Sustainability Report 2023. We have been engaged by DBS Group Holdings (‘the Company” or “DBS") to undertake a limited assurance engagement in respect of the selected sustainability information from the 2023 Sustainability Report of the Company described below for the year ended 31 December 2023 (‘the Identified Sustainability Information’).

stakeholders.Role: Oversees the financial industry in Singapore and initiates sustainability projects.

Source metadata.extraction_context: DBS is a signatory of the industry-led, UN-convened NZBA. As part of our commitment, DBS conducts annual reporting on our performance against our decarbonisation targets in accordance with NZBA guidelines. DBS was the co-chair in the disclosure workstream of the MAS-convened GFIT.

stakeholders.Role: Implements ethical requirements for public accountants and oversees corporate governance standards.

Source metadata.extraction_context: Our firm applies Singapore Standard on Quality Management 1 which requires the firm to design, implement and operate a system of quality management including policies or procedures regarding compliance with ethical requirements, professional standards and applicable legal and regulatory requirements.

stakeholders.Role: Provides sustainability reporting standards that DBS follows for compliance.

Source metadata.extraction_context: The Identified Sustainability Information has been assessed against the Global Reporting Initiative (“GRI”) Sustainability Reporting Standards 2021 (“the Reporting Criteria”).

stakeholders.Role: Represents the employees of DBS Bank in India for collective bargaining on terms of employment.

Source metadata.extraction_context: The DBS Bank India Employee's Union is a registered Trade Union under The Trade Unions Act, 1926. It is affiliated to NCBE (National Confederation of Bank Employees). As of 31 December 2023, a total of 1,004 employees are members of this Union.

stakeholders.Role: Supported the Country Parks Plantation Enrichment Programme

Source metadata.extraction_context: We supported The Conservancy Association (‘CA’) in the Country Parks Plantation Enrichment Programme (‘PEP’), which was launched by the Agriculture, Fisheries and Conservation Department. In June 2022, Bank volunteers helped plant 800 seedlings of native tree species in Tai Lam Country Park.

stakeholders.Stakeholder Name: World Wide Fund for Nature Hong Kong
stakeholders.Canonical Name: World Wide Fund for Nature Hong Kong
stakeholders.Role: Corporate membership support for environmental initiatives

Source metadata.extraction_context: We also continued to provide support to the corporate membership programmes of the World Wide Fund for Nature Hong Kong and Business Environment Council. In addition, we supported Green Power by donating to Green Power Hike for 15 years.

stakeholders.Role: Corporate membership support for environmental initiatives

Source metadata.extraction_context: We also continued to provide support to the corporate membership programmes of the World Wide Fund for Nature Hong Kong and Business Environment Council.

stakeholders.Role: Collaboration on the Hang Seng Low Carbon Future – SolarCare Programme

Source metadata.extraction_context: In collaboration with CarbonCare InnoLab (‘CCIL’), we funded the Hang Seng Low Carbon Future – SolarCare Programme, a project which involved the installation of over 268 solar panels at the Breakthrough Youth Village in 2022.

stakeholders.Role: Implemented the Feed-in Tariff Scheme for renewable energy

Source metadata.extraction_context: The programme will make use of the rebates resulting from the Hong Kong Government’s Feed-in Tariff Scheme to organise various youth climate education activities.

stakeholders.Stakeholder Name: The Community Chest of Hong Kong
stakeholders.Canonical Name: The Community Chest of Hong Kong
stakeholders.Role: Support for charitable events and programmes

Source metadata.extraction_context: Hang Seng has continued to provide support to The Community Chest of Hong Kong (‘the Chest’). We are the Diamond Sponsor of Dress Casual Day for the 25th consecutive year.

stakeholders.Role: Co-organiser of the Hang Seng Table Tennis Academy

Source metadata.extraction_context: Co-organised with the Hong Kong Table Tennis Association, the Hang Seng Table Tennis Academy (‘HSTTA’) identifies and nurtures home-grown table tennis talents through high-quality, structured training sessions.

stakeholders.Stakeholder Name: Hang Seng University of Hong Kong

Source metadata.extraction_context: For example, the Bank’s Executive Director and Chief Executive, Diana Cesar, is the Chairman of the Board of Governors at the Hang Seng University of Hong Kong.
